reasoning: As of the May 5, 2025 knowledge cutoff, there are no indications of an imminent Chinese invasion of Taiwan. A cross-strait invasion involving ground military forces would require massive logistical preparation and troop mobilization visible to US intelligence months in advance. No such buildup has been reported; current US official statements continue to emphasize that a conflict is "neither imminent nor inevitable." With less than two months remaining until the resolution date of July 1, 2025, the logistical impossibility of launching an unobserved invasion within this timeframe pushes the probability to the minimum reasonable threshold.
